item.title: ARTISTS
item.description: Critical profiles that map influence in both directions: who shaped an artist, and whom they shaped.

item.title: INDUSTRY
item.description: The business and physical infrastructure behind recorded music — the labels with an aesthetic identity, the studios whose rooms shaped a sound, and the executives whose decisions determined what got made and released.

item.title: MOMENTS
item.description: Discrete events — a concert, a broadcast, a festival — that changed the direction of music in hours or days rather than years.

item.title: TAXONOMY
item.description: How the music is classified and periodized — the broad eras that frame everything else, the genres and their lineages, and the local scenes where geographic concentration produced something distinctive.

item.title: TECHNIQUES
item.description: The craft layer: the production innovations that changed what records could sound like, and the music-theory concepts that recur as connective tissue across genres, artists, and eras.

item.title: SOURCES
item.description: The scholarly backbone — argument maps of the books and essays the MAP thinks with.

item.title: Bob Dylan
item.description: Genres: folk rock, contemporary folk, singer-songwriter, blues rock, country rock Scene: Greenwich Village folk scene Era: 1961–present (primary period: 1962–1976) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify Bob Dylan arrived in New York in January 1961 as a nineteen-year-old from Hibbing, Minnesota, carrying an ac...

item.title: Brian Wilson
item.description: Genres: Baroque pop, sunshine pop, pop rock, psychedelic pop, progressive pop Scene: Hawthorne / Hollywood, California Era: 1962–2022 (primary period: 1963–1967) RYM · Apple Music · Spotify The opening chord of “God Only Knows” (1966) — an A major with a suspended fourth dropping to a third over a b...
